critical_invariants: System call table layout must match offsets documented in ABI (0x0050-0x00A0 for GPU syscalls)
Function pointer table entries must be 8-byte aligned (64-bit pointers)
b_system dispatch must correctly index into function table using caller-provided subfunction ID
Register preservation across syscall boundaries: RBX, RBP, R12-R15 must be callee-saved
Evolution benchmark must return consistent metrics format for fitness comparison
Debug syscalls must not have side effects on production code paths
Syscall entry must save and restore all caller-saved registers correctly
known_opportunities: Dispatch optimization: replace indirect jump table with direct CMP chain for top-3 hot syscalls
Fast-path for common syscalls: inline b_system_timecounter to avoid function call overhead
Syscall table alignment: align each entry to 8-byte boundary for single-load dispatch
Branch prediction: order CMP chain by frequency to hit common cases first
Eliminate redundant register saves when syscall handler does not clobber callee-saved registers
Evolution benchmark: use RDTSCP for precise cycle counting instead of timer-based measurement
Batch syscall interface: allow multiple syscall requests in single kernel entry
